sourceMovie.title: The Twits
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Twits (2025) stuck because it commits to its animation premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Phil Johnston's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The animation and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Hotel Transylvania: Transformania
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Twits clicked for you, Hotel Transylvania: Transformania (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Twits?
faq.answer: Try Hotel Transylvania: Transformania, The Boxtrolls, The Willoughbys—each shares DNA with The Twits while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Twits?
faq.answer: The Twits (2025) is best described as Animation, Comedy, Family, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Twits worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
